lines.speaker: C. I. Lewis
lines.text: We have seen that □ distributes over implication — the K axiom. And we have seen necessitation: from an empty-context proof we derive necessity.

lines.speaker: Saul Kripke
lines.text: These two rules — box-intro and box-elim — are all we have. No reflexivity, no transitivity. Just the bare structure of worlds and accessibility.

lines.speaker: C. I. Lewis
lines.text: It is remarkable how much follows from so little. The K axiom is not imposed — it emerges.

lines.speaker: Saul Kripke
lines.text: That is the nature of frame conditions. They live in the semantics, not the syntax. The proof rules simply respect the structure of possible worlds.

lines.speaker: C. I. Lewis
lines.text: Onward, then. Let us see what else the bare frame can yield.
